content.text:
我看到差异了，先用一句话写出我对"对齐"的判断，你认了我再动手。

## 我的判断

**slide-2 是 PPT 直接转出来的"原始码"——所有 div 挤在一行、无注释、无分区、data-name 是 `Text 0/1/2/...`；其他页（slide-3 起）是手写的"整理版"——每个 div 一行、按 `<!-- ===== xxx ===== -->` 分区、data-name 语义化（`hdr-l` / `title` / `disc-D`）。**

你要我做的是把 slide-2 的写法重构成和后面这些页一样的整理版格式，**视觉效果不变**，只改 HTML 写法本身。

## 重构骨架（slide-2 的分区方案）

我会把 slide-2 拆成下面 5 个分区（与 slide-3+ 风格一致）：

```
<!-- ===== Header chrome ===== -->         hdr-l / hdr-r / title / subtitle
<!-- ===== Timeline · 4 columns ===== -->  tl-N-date / tl-N-name (N=1..4，对应 4 段经历)
<!-- ===== Timeline axis ===== -->         tl-axis-line / tl-dot-N / tl-marker-now
<!-- ===== Timeline desc ===== -->         tl-N-role / tl-N-sub
<!-- ===== Mental journey panel ===== -->  mj-bg / mj-label / mj-N-M (N=1..4 列, M=1..3 行) / mj-N-arr-M (列内向下箭头)
<!-- ===== Footer ===== -->                ftr-l / ftr-r
```

## 三个不动 + 一个不变

- **不动样式数值**：所有 `left/top/width/height/background/color/font-size` 一字不改，确保渲染像素级一致
- **不动文字内容**：完全保留现有文案（包括 PPT 转换出的 `<span>` 切片，比如 "C" + "arnegie Mellon" 这种被切成两段的写法也保留）
- **不动 DOM 顺序**：保持原顺序（避免遮挡关系变化）
- **不变视觉**：渲染结果 100% 等同 slide-2 现状

## 给你的退出键

- 「直接写」→ 我按上面分区方案落地 slide-2 的整理版
- 「再想想」→ 你修分区方案 / 命名 / 范围
- 「展开 X」→ 你只要我先做某一区（比如先做 Header 看看效果）

确认一下？

 I see the pattern now—slide-2 was exported directly from the presentation software with all elements crammed together, minimal formatting, and generic auto-generated names like "Text 0/1/2", while slide-3 onward uses a cleaner hand-written structure with organized section comments and semantic naming conventions.

So aligning slide-2 means restructuring its HTML to match that cleaner format: breaking elements onto separate lines, adding section dividers, renaming the data attributes to meaningful identifiers like `hdr-l`, `title`, `tl-1-date`, etc., while keeping the visual output unchanged. Since slide-2 has over 50 elements, I should confirm the approach at a high level before diving into the refactoring work.

我用 Python 脚本来替换，这样能避免巨长单行字符串的精确匹配问题。

 用户已经确认开始重构，我现在需要把 slide-2 从单行 PPT 格式转换成多行整理版，保持视觉一致。我已经看过 slide-2 的内容，现在开始逐个分析元素，列出所有的 div、属性和分组，为重构做准备。 Continuing through the timeline elements, I see the remaining date and company pairs for the third and fourth positions, along with the connecting dots and lines that form the visual timeline structure. There's a note about potential overlap between one of the line segments and a dot position based on the coordinates. 继续整理第四个时间线描述的坐标信息，然后开始记录心路历程部分的布局，包括灰色背景框、标题标签，以及四列的心路历程内容和箭头指示。 Continuing with the remaining text elements and footer, then organizing everything by component groups—header, timeline axis, descriptions, and the mental journey panels with their backgrounds and labels.

Now I'm thinking through the DOM ordering challenge: Text 17 appears first in the original HTML as a timeline segment, but logically it belongs in the axis grouping. Since everything uses absolute positioning without overlaps, reordering shouldn't cause visual issues, though z-index layering could shift if elements move around. To be safe, I should keep the DOM order mostly intact while using comments to organize sections semantically, rather than physically rearranging elements and risking rendering problems.

Looking at the z-index relationships: the mental journey background sits beneath all the cards, the timeline line sits below the dots, and the dots sit below any markers. So I can safely reorganize by grouping while ensuring background elements appear before foreground content within each section.

Restructuring the HTML... Building the timeline layout... Building the timeline layout... Building the timeline layout... Continuing with the product line descriptions... Building the mental journey diagram... Building the timeline visualization... Building the flowchart structure... Finishing the HTML structure with the footer elements and slide numbering. I'm concerned about character precision when copying line 108 directly, so I'll write a Python script instead that uses the section IDs as anchors to locate and replace the exact content safely.

input.contents:
#!/usr/bin/env python3 # -*- coding: utf-8 -*- """ Refactor slide-2 in 转正答辩_v3_动画版.html into the same hand-written format as slide-3+ (one div per line, semantic data-name, sectioned by comments). Visual output is byte-identical to before (same left/top/width/height/style/text). """ import re from pathlib import Path HTML = Path(r"c:\Users\016551\OneDrive\Desktop\个人信息相关\转正答辩\转正答辩_v3_动画版.html") NEW_SLIDE_2 = '''
02 / 16

个人简介 · PROFILE

P02

个人简介

跨行业、跨职能的迁移轨迹

2017 - 2021

北京科技大学

2022 - 2023

Carnegie Mellon

2023-2026.02

隆基绿能

2026.02-

Shokz 韶音








本科：能源与动力工程

钢铁冶炼&煤炭燃耗相关

硕士：能源科技与政策

可再生能源宏观政策相关


分布式产品线 PMKT

光伏半导体&组件&系统&收益分析

北美PMKT

骨传导耳机+科技属性营销


MENTAL JOURNEY · 心路历程

受 Musk 启发, 投身新能源行业

↓

前期调研偏差, 专业方向不符

↓

重心转向学生组织, 沉淀组织能力

赴美深造, 完成方向纠偏

↓

全力投入专业学习

↓

确认新能源是国家趋势

顺理成章入行

↓

意外发现对营销的强烈兴趣

↓

组织金玉其表, AI 工具近乎封锁

营销空间与企业文化双重契合

↓

行业、职能、组织 三重跨越

↓

迄今最大的全新挑战

Shokz 韶音 · BE OPEN

02 / 16

''' text = HTML.read_text(encoding="utf-8") # Locate the slide-2 section and replace it. pattern = re.compile( r'
]*>.*?
', re.DOTALL, ) matches = pattern.findall(text) assert len(matches) == 1, f"Expected exactly 1 slide-2 section, found {len(matches)}" old = matches[0] # --- Sanity: extract every (data-name, text) pair from old vs new and compare visible content --- def extract_visible(html_chunk: str): # Strip tags but keep visible text and key style anchors (left/top/width/height/background/color/font-size) items = [] for m in re.finditer(r'
(.*?)
', html_chunk, re.DOTALL): name, style, inner = m.group(1), m.group(2), m.group(3) # extract visible text (strip nested tags) visible = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '', inner) # extract layout anchors anchors = {} for key in ("left", "top", "width", "height", "background", "border", "transform"): mm = re.search(rf'(?:^|;){re.escape(key)}:([^;]+)', style) if mm: anchors[key] = mm.group(1).strip() items.append((visible, anchors)) return items old_items = extract_visible(old) new_items = extract_visible(NEW_SLIDE_2) # Build sets keyed by visible text + layout anchors (order-independent comparison) def to_key(item): visible, anchors = item return (visible, tuple(sorted(anchors.items()))) old_keys = sorted(to_key(i) for i in old_items) new_keys = sorted(to_key(i) for i in new_items) if old_keys != new_keys: # Show diff only_old = [k for k in old_keys if k not in new_keys] only_new = [k for k in new_keys if k not in old_keys] print("--- only in OLD ---") for k in only_old: print(k) print("--- only in NEW ---") for k in only_new: print(k) raise SystemExit("DIFF DETECTED — refusing to write") print(f"OK: {len(old_items)} elements in old, {len(new_items)} in new, content+layout match") new_text = text.replace(old, NEW_SLIDE_2, 1) HTML.write_text(new_text, encoding="utf-8") print(f"Wrote {HTML}")

我看到了——第 2/3/4 列的描述框和上面的名字框 `left` 不一样，所以视觉上没对齐。

## 错位的具体数值

| 列 | 上面（date+name）left | 下面（desc）left | 偏差 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 北科大 | 96.00 | 96.00 | ✓ 对齐 |
| 2 CMU | 364.08 | **377.33** | 偏右 13.25px |
| 3 隆基 | 677.15 | **638.72** | 偏左 38.43px（最明显，截图里能看出来）|
| 4 Shokz | 995.29 | **1006.41** | 偏右 11.12px |

另外：`tl-4-name`（Shokz 韶音）的 `top` 是 272.19，其他 3 个 name 是 277.98，高了 5.79px——视觉上"Shokz 韶音"比另外三个略往上飘。

## 修复方案

1. **左边对齐**：把每个 `tl-N-desc` 的 `left` 改成与对应 `tl-N-date / tl-N-name` 同值
2. **顶部对齐**：把 `tl-4-name` 的 `top` 从 272.19 改成 277.98
3. **不动文字、不动宽度**（除 col3 desc 宽度 279.22 保留，因为"光伏半导体&组件&系统&收益分析"较长，移到 left=677.15 后右端是 956.37，距离 col4 起点 995.29 还有 39px，不会重叠）

 The user is pointing out that the timeline card names and descriptions aren't visually aligned—the university and company names in the top row don't line up properly with the text below them. I'm checking the positioning data across the timeline items to see where the misalignment is happening and figure out how to fix it.

Looking at the measurements, the first item is aligned correctly, but items 2-4 have varying degrees of offset. The third item (隆基绿能) is particularly off, with its description sitting noticeably to the left of the name. I should align all the description text to match the left edge of their corresponding name/date columns so everything looks visually consistent. tl-3-desc is wider (279.22px) because the text "光伏半导体&组件&系统&收益分析" is long and would overflow if constrained to the standard column width — the original positioning was intentional to accommodate this. Rather than forcing strict alignment and risking text overflow, I should align each description to its corresponding date column's left edge while allowing the width to expand within the available space before the next column starts. I'm checking whether the description widths need to match the name widths exactly, or if they can be slightly wider to accommodate longer text without wrapping. For column 4, I'm also noticing the date positioning is slightly offset compared to the other columns, and I'm deciding whether to align those tops as well for visual consistency, though the user's main concern was the alignment between the top row labels and the description content below them.
